name: Tooltip
description: Hover/focus tooltip with arrow and configurable placement.

api.name: Tooltip
api.description: Hover/focus tooltip with arrow and configurable placement.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Extra classes for the tooltip's wrapper element (the relative inline-flex box around the trigger). Merged via Merge so a consumer class wins conflicts without !important — e.g. Class="w-full" makes the wrapper fill its parent so a full-width trigger (e.g. a sidebar nav button) is not shrunk to content width.

records.name: TooltipContext
records.signature: TooltipContext(bool IsOpen, string WrapperId, string ContentId)

examples.title: Side Variations
examples.code:
Tooltip on top

Tooltip on bottom

examples.title: With Icons
examples.code:
Bold

Italic

examples.title: Placement
examples.code:
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Top">Top tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Bottom</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Bottom">Bottom tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Left</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Left">Left tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Right</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Right">Right tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" onto their own single element (which adds the aria-describedby when the tooltip is open). Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element. The key effect is correct a11y: aria-describedby lands on the real focusable control (so a screen reader announces the tooltip on focus) rather than on a non-focusable wrapper <div>. Hover/focus open-close still works via the events bubbling to the Tooltip root. Default false preserves the current inline-flex wrapper exactly.

slots.name: ChildContentSlot
slots.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" onto their own single element (which adds the aria-describedby when the tooltip is open). Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

mdSummary:
# Tooltip

Hover/focus tooltip with arrow and configurable placement.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Overlay
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/tooltip

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tooltip | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| Tooltip | ShowDelay | `int` | `200` |  |
| Tooltip | HideDelay | `int` | `0` |  |
| Tooltip | Class | `string?` | `` | Extra classes for the tooltip's wrapper element (the relative inline-flex box around the trigger). Merged via Merge so a consumer class wins conflicts without !important — e.g. Class="w-full" makes the wrapper fill its parent so a full-width trigger (e.g. a sidebar nav button) is not shrunk to content width. |
| Tooltip | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| TooltipContent | Context | `Tooltip.TooltipContext?` | `` |  |
| TooltipContent | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TooltipContent | Side | `Lumeo.Side` | `Lumeo.Side.Top` |  |
| TooltipContent | ShowArrow | `bool` | `true` |  |
| TooltipContent | Offset | `int` | `8` |  |
| TooltipContent | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| TooltipContent | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| TooltipTrigger | Context | `Tooltip.TooltipContext` | `default!` |  |
| TooltipTrigger | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TooltipTrigger | ChildContentSlot | `RenderFragment<TriggerSlot>?` | `` | Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" onto their own single element (which adds the aria-describedby when the tooltip is open). Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically. |
| TooltipTrigger | AsChild | `bool` | `` | When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element. The key effect is correct a11y: aria-describedby lands on the real focusable control (so a screen reader announces the tooltip on focus) rather than on a non-focusable wrapper <div>. Hover/focus open-close still works via the events bubbling to the Tooltip root. Default false preserves the current inline-flex wrapper exactly. |
| TooltipTrigger | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| TooltipTrigger | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Tooltip) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (TooltipContent) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (TooltipTrigger) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContentSlot` (TooltipTrigger) — Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" onto their own single element (which adds the aria-describedby when the tooltip is open). Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

## Events
- (none)

## Sub-components
- `TooltipContent`, `TooltipTrigger`

## Cascading values it expects
- `Context` (`Tooltip.TooltipContext?`)

## Services it injects
- `IComponentInteropService` (Lumeo.Services)

## Keyboard
- `Escape` — HandleKeyDown handles Escape in Tooltip.razor

## Example
```razor
<Tooltip />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-primary`, `--color-primary-foreground`, `--radius`

## Files
- `UI/Tooltip/Tooltip.razor`
- `UI/Tooltip/TooltipContent.razor`
- `UI/Tooltip/TooltipTrigger.razor`
